title: King's Indian Defense: Classical
summary: Enter the dynamic King's Indian, where Black sacrifices pawn structure for piece activity and kingside attack.
description: The King's Indian is a aggressive defense where Black allows White to build a pawn center, then attacks it with pawn breaks and piece play. You'll learn the key ideas behind this combative opening.
line: 1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 g6 3.Nc3 Bg7 4.e4 d6 5.Nf3 O-O 6.Be2 e5 7.O-O Nc6
objectives: Understand why Black allows White to build a central pawn mass before attacking it.
Learn the typical pawn breaks: ...e5, ...f5, and ...c5 that challenge White's center.
Master the art of counterattacking against a pawn center.
plans: White builds a central pawn wedge and attacks, but must be careful of the coming counterattack.
Black seeks to undermine the center with ...e5 or ...f5, then launch a kingside attack.
The position is complex with both sides having clear plans.
commonMistakes: Do not attack too early. Build up your position first or Black's counterattack comes faster.
Avoid underestimating Black's piece activity. The King's Indian is very dangerous.
Do not let Black's pawn breaks go unchallenged. Meet them with accurate moves.
completionSummary: The King's Indian Defense creates sharp, double-edged positions where both sides have aggressive plans.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.instruction: Start with d4, controlling central space.
steps.coaching: The queen's pawn opening sets up the King's Indian scenario where White builds, Black attacks.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.instruction: The knight develops to f6, attacking e4 and preparing kingside development.
steps.coaching: Standard King's Indian development. The knight controls e4 and prepares ...g6.

steps.title: Expand on the queenside
steps.instruction: Play c4 to control more central space and prepare development.
steps.coaching: This sets up the typical pawn center White wants in the King's Indian.
steps.hint: Push the c-pawn to challenge d4.

steps.title: Black prepares the fianchetto
steps.instruction: Black prepares to fianchetto the bishop on g7.
steps.coaching: This is the key move in the King's Indian. Black will develop the bishop to g7 for kingside pressure.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, developing toward the center.
steps.coaching: Natural development. The knight controls d5 and supports the e4 pawn.
steps.hint: Bring the queen's knight to c3.

steps.title: Black fianchettoes
steps.instruction: The bishop develops to g7, targeting the center and kingside.
steps.coaching: This is the main setup: bishop on g7 gives Black strong diagonal pressure.

steps.title: Build the central pawn wedge
steps.instruction: Play e4, creating the central pawn wedge that Black will attack.
steps.coaching: This is the critical move that defines the King's Indian. White builds a central wall.

steps.title: Black prepares development
steps.instruction: The pawn on d6 supports ...e5 and prepares for kingside castling.
steps.coaching: Standard development. Black is setting up for the coming central breaks.

steps.title: Complete kingside development
steps.instruction: Move Nf3, developing the knight and supporting the e4 pawn.
steps.coaching: White continues development while maintaining the central wedge.

steps.title: Black castles kingside
steps.instruction: Black castles, getting the king safe and connecting the rooks.
steps.coaching: Black is developed enough to castle. The king is now safe for the coming battle.

steps.title: Develop the bishop
steps.coaching: White develops the bishop to a safe square, completing kingside development.

steps.title: Black breaks in the center
steps.instruction: The pawn pushes to e5, challenging the center.
steps.coaching: This is the main idea in the King's Indian: Black attacks the center with pawns.
steps.hint: Push the e-pawn to challenge e4.

steps.title: White castles to safety
steps.instruction: Castle to get the king safe and connect the rooks.
steps.coaching: White castles, completing development. The real battle begins now.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.instruction: The knight develops to c6, supporting the e5 pawn and preparing queenside play.
steps.coaching: Black continues development. The knight also prepares ...d4 to challenge the center.
